topics.summary: Nick's current English content focuses on literary devices, especially imagery and cause-and-effect thinking, using 'There Will Come Soft Rains' as a study text.
readingFocus: Identify examples of imagery in the story.
Notice how descriptions appeal to sight, sound, smell, touch, or taste.
Think about the effect created by each image.
writingTips: When analyzing imagery, explain both the image and the feeling or effect it creates.
Use evidence from the text, then connect it clearly to the author's purpose.
Cause and effect can help explain why a literary device changes the reader's understanding.
structureTemplates: Quote or example -> sense appealed to -> feeling created -> explanation of effect
Point -> evidence -> analysis -> connection to idea

resources.label: There Will Come Soft Rains by Ray Bradbury

practicePrompts: Choose one example of imagery from 'There Will Come Soft Rains' and explain the feeling it creates.
Explain how Ray Bradbury uses imagery to help the reader understand the empty house.
Describe one cause-and-effect relationship in the story and explain why it matters.
